description:
This image displays three panels (a, b, c), each presenting a different geospatial representation of the same unlabelled geographic area, which features a large dark water body, extensive green forested land, and several smaller dark lakes. All three panels share a scale bar below panel (b), marked in segments of 50 m, 100 m, 150 m, 200 m, 250 m, and 300 m. Grid lines are visible across all maps.

Panel (a) shows a natural color satellite image, with the large water body appearing dark blue, vegetated areas in various shades of green, and land surface features visible. A thin light-coloured line is discernible along the coastline.
Panel (b) is a thematic map, where the large water body is depicted in black, and specific land features or a land cover type are highlighted in various shades of green against a white background. The legend defining the green coloration is not present.
Panel (c) is a grayscale image, likely representing terrain relief or a Digital Elevation Model (DEM) derivative, with the water body in dark tones and land areas showing varying shades of grey to indicate surface structure or elevation.

An orange rectangular bounding box highlights an elongated, sinuous land feature across the central part of all three panels, consistent with a valley or a distinctive terrain depression.
